mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate hiring, which boosts investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between employment growth and equity returns
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines in two consecutive months and forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, or if the Rank IC of PAYEMS changes vs returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals an imminent downturn in consumer demand and corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp in 3 months
failure_condition: If after UNRATE trigger condition, forward 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or if the average return after trigger is not significantly negative at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in both housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal a synchronized contraction in construction and manufacturing, amplifying negative economic growth expectations that drive S&P 500 returns downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM decline > 0 AND INDPRO MoM decline > 0
failure_condition: If the combination of HOUST and INDPRO declines does not produce significantly negative returns (t-test p > 0.10) compared to periods without joint decline, or if the average return after trigger is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is tight (UNRATE < 4.0%), a decline in industrial production signals that the economy is hitting supply constraints, reducing corporate output and profits, and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not lead to significantly negative returns (p > 0.10) compared to periods without the condition, or if the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance that lowers the cost of capital and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of FEDFUNDS monthly change vs forward 20-day returns is less than 0.03 over any 2-year window, or if the average return after a decrease is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt weakening of consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenues and profits, pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after trigger condition, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts and rise in unemployment indicates a housing-led economic contraction that spreads through wealth effects and job losses, driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not predict negative returns significantly (p > 0.10) compared to periods without joint move, or if average return positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of stable inflation (year-over-year CPIAUCSL change between 1% and 3%), a month-over-month increase in industrial production indicates robust economic activity without overheating, which boosts investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If during the defined regime, the average forward 5-day return after INDPRO increase is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or if the hit rate is below 55%.
